Update module path for public installation

Co-Authored-By: Codex by OpenAI <codex@openai.com>

Document installation in quick start

Co-Authored-By: Codex by OpenAI <codex@openai.com>

fix(evals): long eval string score persistence in MySQL (#494)

* fix(mysql): allow long eval string scores

Summary:
- widen generation_scores.score_string to use MySQL TEXT
- add schema and MySQL-backed regressions for long string score values

Rationale:
- llm_judge and eval ingest can emit arbitrary string scores that
  overflowed the legacy VARCHAR(255) column and caused Error 1406
- keeping score mapping and query behavior unchanged limits the fix to
  the storage boundary and migration path

Tests:
- go test ./sigil/internal/storage/mysql -run
  'TestGenerationScoreModelUsesTextColumnForStringScores|
  TestScoreToModelKeepsLongStringScores|
  TestEvalStoreScoreCRUDAndLatest|
  TestEvalStorePersistsLongStringScores' -count=1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(mysql): migrate legacy score_string columns

Summary:
- inspect the live generation_scores.score_string column during startup
- explicitly alter legacy VARCHAR(255) columns to MySQL TEXT
- add a migration regression that upgrades a downgraded legacy column

Rationale:
- changing the GORM tag only fixes new table creation and does not
  reliably widen existing MySQL columns in place
- existing deployments hitting Error 1406 need an explicit migration
  step before long string evaluator scores can persist safely

Tests:
- go test ./sigil/internal/storage/mysql -run
  'TestGenerationScoreModelUsesTextColumnForStringScores|
  TestScoreToModelKeepsLongStringScores|
  TestEvalStoreScoreCRUDAndLatest|
  TestEvalStorePersistsLongStringScores|
  TestAutoMigrateUpgradesLegacyGenerationScoreStringColumn' -count=1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(elixir): reduce default workflow concurrency

Summary:
- lower the sample Elixir workflow concurrency from 50 to 5 agents

Rationale:
- keep the default rollout more conservative for the current Taiga
  orchestration run
- reduce immediate slot saturation and make the first unattended run
  easier to observe

Tests:
- make -C elixir all

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(elixir): raise default workflow agent capacity

Summary:
- increase the sample Elixir workflow concurrency from 10 to 50 agents
- increase the default max turns per agent run from 20 to 250

Rationale:
- support larger unattended Symphony runs without immediately
  saturating the orchestrator on bigger ticket batches
- keep the local default workflow closer to the high-throughput
  use case we were configuring for the Taiga rollout

Tests:
- make -C elixir all

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(github): fall back when pr edits need project scope

Summary:
- add a fallback in `github/scripts/prs_update.sh` that retries title,
  body, and base updates through `gh api` when `gh pr edit` fails with
  `missing required scopes [read:project]`
- keep the helper output concise by reporting a short PR summary after a
  successful fallback update
- document the fallback behavior in `github/SKILL.md` and the GitHub
  skill references for script usage and workflow guidance

Rationale:
- `gh pr edit` can now fail on simple metadata updates when the active
  token lacks project-read scope, even though the underlying PR update
  is otherwise allowed
- the helper should preserve the narrow repo-scoped workflow while
  still succeeding for common body/title/base edits in that auth state

Tests:
- bash -n github/scripts/prs_update.sh
- github/scripts/check_docs_script_refs.sh --skill-dir github
- github/scripts/prs_update.sh --pr 37 --body "<existing body>" --repo
  ambrogio-dev/livekit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(orchestrator): stabilize Codex run handoff

Summary:
- wire Linear state transitions into the runtime and configure dispatch
  and handoff states from WORKFLOW.md
- bootstrap issue workspaces, expose Codex stderr and selective runtime
  diagnostics, and reduce noisy streaming logs
- stop agent runs after a single completed turn so the orchestrator can
  move active issues to In Review without looping

Rationale:
- state changes must be owned by the orchestrator boundary instead of
  relying on prompt compliance or internal runner continuation loops
- richer diagnostics were needed to distinguish auth errors, timeouts,
  and protocol progress during live Codex sessions
- one-turn attempts avoid repeated work while still allowing the
  orchestrator to schedule explicit follow-up passes through workflow
  state

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(plugin): make saved conversation ordering explicit (#448)

* refactor(plugin): make saved conversation ordering explicit

Replace implicit reverse-based ordering in GenerationPicker with explicit created_at descending sorting to avoid incorrect UI order when API response order changes.

Add focused unit tests for ordering, non-mutation, and invalid timestamp handling.

* fix: guard against NaN in timestamp comparator tie-breaking

When both timestamps are invalid, toTimestamp returns NEGATIVE_INFINITY
for each, and the subtraction yields NaN. Since NaN !== 0 is true in JS,
the comparator returned NaN instead of falling through to the intended
index-based tie-breaker. Add an explicit Number.isNaN guard so that
invalid-timestamp ties correctly reach the deterministic index fallback.

Applied via @cursor push command

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(harness): harden closeout readiness and landing

Summary:
- add a shared repository-readiness preflight and fail publish/final
  gate early on missing checks, disabled Actions, policy mismatch,
  stale base state, and invalid branch state
- retain the latest passing review, ci-status, and final-gate bundle
  under .local/final-evidence and keep tracked docs summary-first
- add worktree-aware land_merge behavior plus regression coverage for
  remote-merge-success warnings and merge-policy enforcement

Rationale:
- close issues #31, #32, and #34 as one harness slice without leaving
  cleanup, readiness, or landing semantics ambiguous
- make the repository record durable and easy to scan while still
  leaving one obvious local evidence bundle for deeper inspection
- reduce the chance of follow-up issues by tightening negative-path
  coverage before publish and final gate

commit.message:
fix: buffer non-streaming response body for token reconciliation (closes #12) (#20)

* fix: buffer non-streaming response body for token reconciliation (closes #12)

Without a body_filter phase for non-streaming responses, cost_extractor
was never called and pessimistic TPM/TPD reservations were never refunded,
causing token budgets to drain faster than actual usage.

Changes:
- streaming.lua: import cost_extractor; add _reconcile_non_streaming() that
  buffers up to 1 MiB of response body per spec, extracts usage.total_tokens,
  and calls llm_limiter.reconcile() to refund unused tokens
- streaming.lua: body_filter() now handles the non-streaming path (active=false
  but key present) by buffering chunks and reconciling on EOF
- streaming.lua: init_stream() initialises body_buffer field
- streaming_spec.lua: install mock_cjson_safe; add 4 new scenarios covering
  full-body extraction, chunked delivery, missing usage field (no over-refund),
  and no-context passthrough

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(e2e): add token reconciliation e2e test suite (closes #12)

Add mock LLM backend (nginx serving fixed usage JSON), a new edge service
in reverse_proxy mode with token_bucket_llm policy, and three e2e scenarios:

- pass-through: response body contains usage.total_tokens=50
- metric: fairvisor_token_reservation_unused_total emitted after reconcile
- refund: 5 consecutive requests succeed (reconciled ~50 tokens each vs
  pessimistic 1000, so budget is not exhausted)

Test runs as part of the nightly full e2e suite (pytest tests/e2e).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: two pre-existing bugs that prevented token_bucket_llm from working e2e

1. bundle_loader: validate_config was called on a shallow copy of
   algorithm_config, so computed fields (_tpm_bucket_config, default
   defaults etc.) were never written back to rule.algorithm_config.
   After validation, merge all non-algorithm keys back so request-time
   code sees the normalised config.

2. rule_engine: the final "allow" decision built at line 882 omitted
   rule_name, and limit_result.key was never set for token_bucket_llm.
   decision_api.lua:933 fell through to the fallback which concatenated
   nil rule_name, causing a 500.  Fix: track last_allow_rule_name, set
   limit_result.key = counter_key for allowed LLM checks, and include
   rule_name in the final allow decision.

These bugs were latent and exposed by the new e2e test suite added for
issue #12 / Feature 015.  All 3 e2e reconciliation tests now pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add initial Go SDK conformance harness slice (#458)

* test(go-sdk): add initial conformance harness slice

Summary:
- add a public-API Go conformance harness with localhost gRPC ingest,
  rating capture, span recording, and manual metric collection
- cover conversation title, user ID, and agent identity semantics in
  `package sigil_test` with table-driven conformance scenarios
- update the active execution plan to reflect the shipped harness slice

Rationale:
- establish a black-box reference harness that other SDK conformance
  suites can copy without relying on in-package test seams
- lock the first high-signal identity semantics against raw exported
  data before expanding into roundtrip and provider/framework coverage

Tests:
- go test -run TestConformance -count=1 ./sdks/go/sigil/...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(go-sdk): keep conformance telemetry assertions alive

Summary:
- remove dead harness helpers that tripped golangci-lint in CI
- flush generation export explicitly in the conformance helper before
  making assertions
- keep metric and tracer providers alive until test cleanup so metric
  collection works after the client shutdown gate

Rationale:
- align the local conformance harness lifecycle with the CI lint and
  test expectations instead of relying on teardown ordering
- keep the first Go conformance slice green on the branch PR checks

Tests:
- go test -run TestConformance -count=1 ./sdks/go/sigil/...
- (cd sdks/go && golangci-lint run ./...)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(go-sdk): keep conformance assertions CI-clean

Summary:
- reorder the Go conformance assertions so spans and metrics are
  checked before shutting down the harness
- keep the proto export assertions after shutdown so the gRPC worker
  has drained deterministically
- remove the now-unneeded flush path from the test helper

Rationale:
- fix the red lint/format workflow without dropping the metric helper
  coverage from the first conformance slice
- make the harness deterministic for both metric collection and gRPC
  export capture under CI timing

Tests:
- go test -run TestConformance ./sdks/go/sigil/...
- go test ./sdks/go/sigil/...
- golangci-lint run ./sdks/go/...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Improve Symphony local runtime and UI proof workflows (#454)

* chore(symphony): add repo workflow and worker skills

Summary:
- add the Symphony workflow file for this repo and point it at the new Linear project
- install repo-local worker skills, including a Sigil-specific launch-app skill
- teach the land skill to treat Cursor Bugbot feedback as blocking until rerun clean

Rationale:
- Symphony workers need repo-local workflow and skill definitions in remote clones
- the Sigil stack needs explicit runtime launch instructions for validation
- merge shepherding must handle Bugbot findings instead of merging on green CI alone

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile .agents/skills/land/land_watch.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(symphony): add installation guide

Summary:
- add a dedicated reference guide for Symphony setup in the Sigil repo
- link the guide from the references index and top-level docs index
- document the committed workflow, Linear project, required states, and launch command

Rationale:
- other contributors need a single canonical path to reproduce the Symphony setup
- the repo now contains workflow and skill files that deserve explicit operational docs
- the launch flow requires repo-specific Linear and runtime details that were not documented

Tests:
- not run (docs only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat(symphony): improve local runtime and proof workflows

Summary:
- add worktree-safe runtime helpers, lighter docker tasks, and cleanup hooks
- add UI proof capture/upload tooling and a repo-local ui-proof skill
- render personal Symphony workflow config locally instead of tracking a
  developer-specific Linear project slug in git
- document the personal-project workflow and tighten agent guidance for
  self-improvement and runtime validation

Rationale:
- make Symphony practical for multiple developers without shared project slug
  conflicts or fixed localhost port collisions
- give workers a concrete way to validate UI changes and attach proof to
  Linear instead of relying on text-only handoff
- reduce leftover workspace runtime resources by cleaning Compose projects on
  workspace removal

Tests:
- bash -n scripts/bootstrap-env.sh scripts/render-symphony-workflow.sh
  scripts/run-sigil-worktree.sh .config/devex/sdk-traffic/run-all.sh
- node --check apps/plugin/scripts/capture-ui-proof.mjs
  apps/plugin/scripts/ensure-grafana-auth.mjs
  apps/plugin/scripts/grafana-playwright-auth.mjs
  apps/plugin/scripts/upload-linear-assets.mjs
- python3 -m py_compile .agents/skills/land/land_watch.py
- mise tasks | rg 'workflow:symphony:render|up:worktree|down:worktree:destroy|capture:ui-proof|ensure:grafana:auth'
- mise run ensure:grafana:auth
- mise run capture:ui-proof

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(symphony): address bugbot and format issues

Pass the PR head SHA into the land watcher review loop so human feedback checks do not fail at runtime. Reuse the shared review pagination helper and remove the committed personal workflow path from the Symphony setup docs.

Also apply the package-local Prettier formatting that the plugin CI check expects for the Playwright helper scripts.

* fix(symphony): preserve compose keys after port filtering

Share the compose YAML service-key matching used by the worktree runtime scripts so scalar-valued keys like restart, working_dir, and image correctly terminate a ports block. Add a regression test covering the dropped-key case and keep the assistant offset script aligned with the same behavior.

* fix(symphony): make compose helpers cwd-independent

Pass the repo path into the inline Python helpers so they can import shared compose parsing logic even when the shell script is launched from another working directory. Rename the shared regex to reflect that it intentionally marks the end of a ports block in both runtime scripts.

* fix(sdk-traffic): fall back to system python3 for one-shot assertions

When the python emitter is disabled via SIGIL_TRAFFIC_EMITTERS, setup_python
is skipped and the venv at PYTHON_VENV is never created. run_one_shot_assertions
unconditionally used the venv python binary, causing a runtime error.

The assertion script (assert-one-shot.py) only uses stdlib modules, so it
does not need the venv. Fall back to system python3 when the venv binary
does not exist.

Applied via @cursor push command

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
feat(skills): 拆分 git 提交规范为独立 skill (#32)

## Why
- 将 git 提交相关规范从 `AGENTS.md` 中拆出，减少全局规则体积
- 把提交/推送授权边界、提交信息与分支命名约定集中到单一 skill，便于复用

## What
- 新增 `git-commit` skill，收拢 commit、push、branch、message、trailer 等约定
- 精简 `AGENTS.md` 中分散的 git 规则，改为在相关场景下引用该 skill
- 调整章节编号，保留原有依赖与其他通用规则

## Testing
- 已检查 `AGENTS.md` 与 `skills/git-commit/SKILL.md` 的 diff
- 已确认新 skill 覆盖原先 git 相关规则，且 `AGENTS.md` 仅保留入口说明

---------

commit.message:
fix LLM summary truncation on token-limited completions

Reasoning models can spend output budget on internal thought tokens, then stop
with `length`/`max_tokens`; we treated that partial text as success, causing
one-line summaries.

∙ remove the tight summary max-token heuristic and trust prompt length guidance
∙ retry once when finish_reason signals token-limit truncation
∙ reject safety/content-filter/recitation style finish reasons explicitly
∙ use Google SDK `resp.Text()` to avoid leaking thought parts in final output
∙ keep `<think>` cleanup as a fallback for providers that emit raw think blocks

Co-Authored-By: Gemini <noreply@deepmind.google>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- 新增 /打开项目管理页面 命令并兼容 /打开web 别名，统一私聊触发限制与 token+入口返回

- 同步更新智能路由白名单、命令注解、意图词表与正样本，保持 slfk 路由可识别

- 补齐命令解析与链接构造回归测试，修复私聊判定未知来源默认放行问题（fail-closed）

- 升级版本号到 0.34.0，并更新 CHANGELOG 与 README 命令说明

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: preserve deemphasized color in replace plan metadata

Upgrade and downgrade plan lines rendered the `from <pkgid>` segment with a
colored pkgid string that reset the terminal color to default. That caused
the remainder of the parenthetical metadata, including the trailing `)`, to
lose the intended deemphasized color.

Render replace metadata incrementally and reapply `Color::Deemphasize`
immediately after the colored `from` pkgid. Add a regression covering the
color reset boundary.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
